evals.description: Resolve git author and GitHub login dynamically — no hardcoded usernames
evals.prompt: Run /report-out on a fresh machine where the user is configured but not as 'WilcoLouwerse'.
evals.setup: git config user.name returns 'Jane Doe'; gh api user returns {login: 'janedoe123'}.
evals.expected_output: Skill uses Jane Doe / janedoe123 throughout — no references to WilcoLouwerse.
expectations: Skill calls 'git config user.name' to obtain the author
Skill calls 'gh api user' to obtain the GitHub login
Skill uses $HOME (not /home/<name>) for repo discovery
Skill does NOT contain hardcoded 'WilcoLouwerse' or '/home/wilco' references in SKILL.md or references/

evals.description: Before posting a tracking-issue comment, check for an existing comment within the last 24 hours and offer to edit
evals.prompt: Run /report-out for today; tracking issue #20 has a comment from the user posted 4 hours ago.
evals.setup: gh api repos/.../issues/20/comments returns a comment by the user with created_at = 4 hours ago.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects the recent comment and asks the user whether to edit it instead of posting a new one.
expectations: Skill computes a 24h-ago timestamp
Skill queries existing comments and filters to (user.login == GH_LOGIN, created_at > 24h-ago)
Skill presents an AskUserQuestion offering Edit / New / Skip when a recent comment exists
Skill uses gh api repos/.../issues/comments/{id} -X PATCH (not new POST) when user chooses Edit

evals.description: Skill always ends with a copy-paste Dutch report-out message containing PRs the user authored or merged today
evals.prompt: Run /report-out at end-of-day. User authored PR #1394 (open) and merged PR #1364 today.
evals.setup: gh search prs returns 1394 (open) and 1364 (merged) as user-authored.
evals.expected_output: Skill ends with a fenced markdown block: bare 'Report out:' heading (plain text, trailing colon, no emoji or date — Slack already shows the timestamp), summary bullets, PR list with bare URLs and status emoji, copy-paste-ready.
expectations: Final output contains the exact 'Report out:' heading on its own line (plain text, trailing colon)
Heading is bare — no emoji (🗓️/spiral_calendar_pad), no bold, no day name, no date suffix
PR list uses bare URLs (https://github.com/...) — no 'org/repo #N' shorthand (Slack does not auto-link that form)
Final output lists user-authored PRs with status emoji (✅ for merged, 🟡 for open)
Final output is wrapped in a fenced markdown block for easy copy-paste
Final output does NOT include PRs the user did not author or merge

evals.description: Skill asks for additional user input both at the start (before drafting) and at the end (before final report-out)
evals.prompt: Run /report-out and provide context 'today was mostly review work' at the start.
evals.setup: User has commits + 2 open PRs; tracking issues exist.
evals.expected_output: Skill asks for additional input at Step 1 (start) and Step 9 (end). Captured context shapes the report.
expectations: Skill asks for additional input at the start, before any drafting
Skill asks for additional input at the end, before producing the final report-out
Captured user context is incorporated into issue-comment drafts and the final message
User can opt out of issue/PR updates and skip directly to the report-out

evals.description: Skill auto-discovers issues and PRs the user has interacted with today via gh search, presented as recommended baseline
evals.prompt: Run /report-out without specifying issues or PRs.
evals.setup: User has 3 PRs and 2 issue comments today across 2 repos.
evals.expected_output: Skill discovers all 5 items via gh search and presents them as defaults; user can override.
expectations: Skill calls 'gh search prs --author @me --updated >=$DATE'
Skill calls 'gh search issues --commenter @me --updated >=$DATE'
Skill deduplicates by (repo, number)
Skill presents discovered items in the overview before asking for direction
Skill allows the user to add or remove items from the discovered list

evals.description: Detect branches with today's commits but no open PR and suggest creating one
evals.prompt: Run /report-out — feature/foo has 3 commits today, no open PR, branch is pushed to origin.
evals.setup: git log shows today's commits on feature/foo; gh pr list --head feature/foo --state open returns empty.
evals.expected_output: Skill flags the branch and asks the user whether to create a PR; defaults to /create-pr delegation.
expectations: Skill iterates branches with today's commits
Skill skips default branches (main/master/development/beta/staging)
Skill calls 'gh pr list --head $BRANCH --state open' to check existence
Skill surfaces the suggestion via AskUserQuestion before any action
Skill prefers delegating to /create-pr over direct gh api POST
Skill never pushes the branch without explicit authorization phrase

evals.description: Detect issues whose work appears done and suggest closing them with state_reason
evals.prompt: Run /report-out — issue #20 has all linked PRs merged in last 7 days and a fresh user comment today.
evals.setup: gh api ...issues/20/comments shows merged-PR URLs; user posted today.
evals.expected_output: Skill tags issue with 'close-suggested' and asks the user whether to close.
expectations: Skill scans last-7-days comments for PR URLs
Skill verifies each linked PR is merged
Skill detects the closing-trailer pattern in recent user comments
Skill asks Yes/No with completed vs not_planned options
Skill calls 'gh api repos/.../issues/N -X PATCH -f state=closed' only after user approval

evals.description: Suggest creating a follow-up issue when an issue is older than 5 days, has many comments, and remains open
evals.prompt: Run /report-out — issue #14 is 8 days old with 15 comments, still open, no closing-trailer.
evals.setup: Issue created 8 days before TARGET_DATE; comment count = 15; state = open.
evals.expected_output: Skill tags 'stale-and-busy' and offers to draft a follow-up issue referencing the parent.
expectations: Skill computes age in days and counts comments
Skill applies 5-day + 10-comment thresholds
Skill offers a follow-up draft that references the parent (Volgt op #N)
Skill draft body summarizes done work + open work
Skill creates new issue via 'gh api repos/.../issues -X POST' only after approval
Skill offers to post a closing-trailer on the parent pointing to the new follow-up

evals.description: Detect uncommitted changes per repo and offer commit / stash / push / skip with explicit confirmations
evals.prompt: Run /report-out — repo X on feature/foo has 3 modified files and 1 untracked file.
evals.setup: git status --short returns 4 entries; user has commits today on the same branch.
evals.expected_output: Skill surfaces the file list, asks Commit / Stash / Skip, and only commits/pushes after explicit Yes.
expectations: Skill detects uncommitted changes via 'git status --short' in Step 4
Skill surfaces them in the Step 6 overview as a separate section
Step 8.4 lists the exact files (modified, staged, untracked) before asking
Skill offers Commit (drafted message) / Commit (user-provided) / Stash WIP / Skip via AskUserQuestion
Skill never auto-commits — runs 'git commit' only after explicit Yes
Skill offers a separate push prompt after a successful commit; respects the git-push authorization phrase
Skill never uses 'git commit --no-verify' without explicit user authorization

should_trigger: give me a report-out for today
summarize my git activity for today
update my tracking issue with today's progress
what PRs did I open today and what's their status
post my daily progress to the tracking issue
should_not_trigger: fix a bug in the cart
add a new product to the seed data
write a commit message for this change
